methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Locus Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Augury is a Series F robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Gal Shaul and Saar Yoskovitz. The target has raised $75M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 comparable companies: 1 public (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including Locus Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or valuation-tier overlap to support reliable median comps methodology; only 1–2 datapoints exist within applicable filters.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable technique. Using the $75M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement yields an implied valuation of $98M. This method reflects the expectation that frontier-tech robotics firms command moderate step-ups between institutional funding rounds at this maturity level.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $83M to $112M, centered on a median of $98M. The range reflects ±15% sensitivity around stage-progression outcomes. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-peer revenue or growth multiples, which limits cross-validation, and the heterogeneity of business models and deployment timelines within the broader robotics sector. A more precise valuation would require disclosure of Augury's current revenue run-rate or customer concentration metrics relative to accessible peer financials.
